member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IFlashUtil
member.summary: Defines an interface for interacting with Flash-based ActionScript objects, enabling calls to SWF functions, object manipulation, and data retrieval or conversion between ActionScript and .NET types.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide methods to invoke Flash functions, access or modify Flash objects by path, and convert data between Flash XML and .NET representations. The interface inherits from , so implementations may hold unmanaged resources that should be released when no longer needed.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.ICaptureProxy
member.summary: Defines the contract for a network capture proxy that relays traffic to a specified destination and supports packet interception.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow inspection and modification of network traffic via registered interceptors. The proxy can be started and stopped, and notifies listeners of property changes through the interface.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IOptionContainer
member.summary: Defines a contract for managing a collection of configurable options, including retrieving, setting, loading, and saving option values, as well as supporting option categories and user configuration interfaces.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide mechanisms for accessing and modifying option values by name, category, or option definition. The interface supports loading and saving option values to persistent storage, resetting options to their default values, and presenting a user interface for option configuration. Option values can be retrieved and set in strongly typed form, with support for multiple categories of options.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IPluginManager
member.summary: Defines methods and properties for managing the lifecycle and access of plugins within the application.
member.remarks: The IPluginManager interface provides functionality to load, unload, and retrieve information about plugins at runtime. It enables dynamic management of plugins, allowing for extensibility and modularity in the application. Implementations are responsible for handling plugin discovery, loading from assemblies, and maintaining references to plugin containers.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.ISkuaPlugin
member.summary: Defines the contract for a Skua plugin, including metadata, lifecycle methods, and configuration options.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to create a plugin that can be loaded and managed by the Skua plugin system. The interface provides properties for plugin identification and description, as well as methods for handling plugin initialization and cleanup. Plugins can expose configurable options and specify a unique storage key for their settings.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptAuras
member.summary: Defines methods and properties for querying and retrieving aura effects applied to a subject.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide access to information about active auras, including their presence, stack counts, durations, and retrieval by name or pattern. This interface is typically used in systems where entities can have multiple status effects (auras) that may influence behavior or attributes.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSelfAuras
member.summary: Defines functionality for managing auras that are applied to the player.
member.remarks: This interface extends to provide operations specific to self-applied auras. Implementations may offer additional methods or properties for handling auras that affect the player itself, as opposed to those applied to others.

member.summary: Represents a collection of aura-related operations that can be performed on a target entity.
member.remarks: This interface extends to provide aura management functionality specific to the target entity. Implementations may offer additional context or behaviors relevant to the target entity within a scripting environment.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptCombat
member.summary: Defines methods and properties for controlling combat actions in a scriptable environment, including targeting, attacking, and managing combat state.
member.remarks: This interface provides a standardized set of combat-related operations for use in scripting scenarios, such as automating player actions or implementing custom combat logic. Implementations are expected to handle targeting, attacking monsters or players, and managing the player's combat state.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHunt
member.summary: Defines methods for hunting monsters in the current map, including targeting by name, ID, or instance, collecting items from monsters, and prioritizing hunts based on specified criteria.
member.remarks: The IScriptHunt interface provides a set of methods to automate monster hunting and item collection tasks. Methods support various ways to specify targets (by name, ID, or Monster instance) and allow for item farming with quantity and temporary item options. Some methods accept a cancellation token to support cooperative cancellation. Priority-based hunting enables advanced control over which monsters are targeted first, using the specified HuntPriorities.

member.summary: Looks for the monster with specified name in the current map and kills it. This method disregards ScriptOptions#HuntPriority.
member.param: The name of the enemy to hunt.

member.param: The name of the enemy to hunt.
A cancellation token.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHunt.WithPriority(System.String,Skua.Core.Models.HuntPriorities)
member.summary: Hunts monsters with a priority. If there is no priority, this has the same behaviour as just Hunt. If a priority is specified, monsters in the map are sorted by the given priority. Once sorted, the monster in the current cell which best matches the priority is killed. Otherwise, a cell jump is awaited and done based on ScriptOptions#HuntDelay.
member.param: The name of the monster to hunt
The priority which the hunts will be ordered

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptKill
member.summary: Defines methods for automating combat actions, including attacking players or monsters and repeatedly defeating targets to collect specified items or quantities. Intended for use in scripting scenarios that require programmatic control over in-game combat and item farming.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface may respect timing or safety constraints (such as 'SafeTimings') to ensure actions are performed reliably. Methods that accept a cancellation token allow the operation to be cancelled before completion. These methods are typically used in automated gameplay scripts for player-versus-player (PVP) or player-versus-environment (PVE) scenarios, such as farming items or completing quests.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptKill.Player(System.String)
member.summary: Attacks the player with specified and waits until they are killed (if SafeTimings are enabled). This should only be used in PVP.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHandlers
member.summary: Defines methods for registering, managing, and removing script handlers that execute actions at specified intervals or conditions within a scripting environment.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow scripts to schedule actions to run periodically or once after a delay, using a tick-based timing system (where one tick equals 20 milliseconds). Handlers can be uniquely named for identification and management. This interface is typically used to coordinate timed or recurring script logic, such as scheduled tasks or event polling.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHandlers.CurrentHandlers
member.summary: A list holding currently registered handlers.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHandlers.RegisterHandler(System.Int32,System.Func{Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptInterface,System.Boolean},System.String)
member.summary: Register an to be executed every time the specified number of has passed. A tick is 20ms.
member.param: Number of ticks between consecutive executions of the action.
Action to carry out. If this function returns false, the handler will be removed.
Name of this handler (must be unique). Passing null will assign it a unique name.
member.returns: The registered.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHandlers.RegisterHandler(System.Int32,System.Action{Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptInterface},System.String)
member.summary: Register an to be executed every time the specified number of has passed. A tick is 20ms.
member.param: Number of ticks between consecutive executions of the action.
Action to carry out at every interval
Name of this handler (must be unique). Passing null will assign it a unique name.
member.returns: The registered.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptHandlers.RegisterOnce(System.Int32,System.Action{Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptInterface},System.String)
member.summary: Register an to be executed every time the specified number of has passed. A tick is 20ms.
member.param: Number of ticks between consecutive executions of the action.
Action to be executed once.
Name of this handler (must be unique). Passing null will assign it a unique name.
member.returns: The registered.

member.summary: Removes the handler with specified name.
member.param: Name of the handler to remove.
member.returns: if the handler was removed.

member.summary: Removes the specified handler.
member.param: Handler to remove.
member.returns: if the handler was removed.

member.summary: Removes all the specified handlers.

member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow equipping individual items or multiple items at once, including items that are usable in a specific slot. member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptBank
member.summary: Defines the contract for interacting with a scriptable bank, providing methods to load, open, and transfer items between the bank and inventory, as well as to check and manage inventory contents.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface enable scripts to automate bank operations, such as moving items to and from the inventory, ensuring item transfers, and handling bank state. The interface extends and management capabilities, allowing for comprehensive inventory control within scripts.

member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide functionality for storing and equipping script items. This interface extends , indicating that it supports equipping operations.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptTempInv
member.summary: Represents a temporary inventory for script operations that supports inventory checking functionality.
member.remarks: This interface extends , enabling scripts to query or validate the presence of items in a temporary inventory context. Implementations may be used for transient or script-driven item management scenarios.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptAuto
member.summary: Defines the contract for controlling automated attack and hunt features, including starting, stopping, and monitoring the running state.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide mechanisms to automate attack or hunt actions in a game context. The interface supports both synchronous and asynchronous stopping operations, making it suitable for integration with UI components that require non-blocking behavior. The interface also notifies listeners of property changes via the interface, allowing consumers to react to changes in the running state.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptAuto.IsRunning

member.param: The name of the class to use.
The to use the class.
Optional array of MapIDs to target with priority order.

member.summary: Starts the Auto Hunt function. The player will hunt the current target or all the monsters in the current room throughout the map.
member.param: The name of the class to use.
The to use the class.
Optional array of MapIDs to target with priority order.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptAuto.Stop
member.summary: Stops the Auto Attack/Hunt.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptAuto.StopAsync
member.summary: Stops the Auto Attack/Hunt asynchronously, use for UI elements.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptBoost
member.summary: Defines the contract for managing and applying various boost types, such as class, experience, gold, and reputation boosts, including their activation, identification, and timer control.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow clients to enable or disable boost timers, retrieve and set boost IDs, check boost status, and control which boost types are in use. The interface also provides methods to search for available boosts in the player's inventory or bank, and to apply boosts as needed. Property change notifications are provided via the interface, enabling data binding scenarios.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptBotStats
member.summary: Represents a set of statistics related to script bot activity, including counts of deaths, kills, drops, quests, and re-logins.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide real-time tracking of key gameplay events for a script bot. Properties are typically updated as the bot performs actions in the game. Changes to property values raise the PropertyChanged event, allowing clients to observe updates. The Reset method resets all statistics to their initial state.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptDrop
member.summary: Defines the contract for managing and interacting with item drops, including querying available drops, configuring pickup behavior, and performing pickup or rejection operations.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow scripts or applications to automate the process of collecting or rejecting item drops, with support for configurable pickup lists, intervals, and filtering options. The interface provides both synchronous and asynchronous control methods, as well as events for property change notifications via . Thread safety and specific timing behaviors may depend on the implementation.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptEvent
member.summary: Defines an interface for subscribing to script-related events in the game, such as player actions, quest progress, map changes, and item transactions.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide event notifications for various in-game occurrences, allowing scripts to respond to player activity, game state changes, and system events. Event handlers can be attached to react to specific scenarios, such as when a player logs out, completes a quest, or receives an item drop. Some events may require special handling; for example, after a map change, it is recommended to add a delay before invoking map-related methods to ensure the game state is fully updated.

member.name: E:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptEvent.Logout
member.summary: Occurs when the player log out of the game.

member.name: E:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptEvent.PlayerDeath

member.summary: Occurs when a quest is accepted.

member.name: E:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptEvent.QuestTurnedIn
member.summary: Occurs when a quest is turned.

member.name: E:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptEvent.MapChanged
member.summary: Occurs when the current map changes.
member.remarks:
Note that the is fired when you send the join command.
Before using any method in this event, be sure to add a delay.

member.name: E:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptEvent.CellChanged

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptFaction
member.summary: Represents a collection of player faction reputation data and provides methods to query faction ranks and reputation status.
member.remarks: Use this interface to access the list of factions in which the player has earned reputation, retrieve the player's rank within a specific faction, or check if the player meets a required rank for a given faction. Factions can be referenced by either their name or unique identifier. Implementations should ensure that faction lookups are consistent and that rank queries reflect the player's current reputation state.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptInterface
member.summary: Defines the contract for the main scripting interface, providing access to core bot services, game automation features, and script management utilities.
member.remarks: The IScriptInterface exposes a comprehensive set of properties and methods for interacting with game elements, managing script execution, and customizing bot behavior. It serves as the primary entry point for scripts, aggregating functionality such as combat automation, inventory management, quest handling, event monitoring, and user interface interactions. Implementations of this interface are expected to be thread-safe only if explicitly documented. Consumers should use the provided properties and methods to interact with the game environment and control script flow, rather than accessing underlying implementations directly.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptInventoryHelper
member.summary: Provides methods for checking the presence and quantity of items across player, bank, temporary, and house inventories.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface enable scripts to verify whether specific items exist in sufficient quantity in any supported inventory location. Methods may optionally move items to the player's main inventory if requested. These checks are useful for automating inventory management tasks, such as ensuring required items are available before performing actions or quests.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptLite
member.summary: Defines an interface for managing and querying various gameplay and user interface options, including animation toggles, UI visibility, and advanced option retrieval and assignment.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide programmatic access to a range of settings that control visual effects, user interface elements, and gameplay behaviors. These options are typically used to customize the user experience, optimize performance, or automate certain actions. The interface also includes generic methods for retrieving and setting advanced options by name, allowing for flexible extension and integration with option panels.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptMonster
member.summary: Provides methods and properties for accessing and querying monsters present in the current cell and map, including their locations, availability, and status.
member.remarks: The interface enables scripts to retrieve information about monsters in the current game context, such as which monsters are present, which can be attacked, and their distribution across map cells. It also provides methods to check for the existence of monsters by name or ID, obtain summaries of monster auras, and attempt to retrieve specific monster instances.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption
member.summary: Represents a configurable set of script options and user preferences for automation scripts, providing access to option values and methods.
member.remarks: manages a collection of script-related settings, including user preferences and automation behaviors. This class is intended to be used as a central point for script configuration and is designed to integrate with settings services and messaging systems for state management.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.OptionDictionary
member.summary: Gets an immutable dictionary that maps option names to factory functions for their values.
member.remarks: Each entry associates a string key representing the option name with a delegate that returns the option's value when invoked. The dictionary is read-only and cannot be modified after creation.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.ReloginTryDelay
member.summary: Delay between re-login tries using .

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.LoginTimeout
member.summary: Timeout

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AcceptACDrops
member.summary: When enabled will pick up any AC item that drops, even when the drop should be rejected.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AcceptAllDrops
member.summary: When enabled will pick up any item that drops.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.RejectAllDrops
member.summary: When enabled will reject all items that drops (if is will first accept AC items and then reject).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AggroAllMonsters
member.summary: Determines whether all monsters in the MAP should be provoked. They will all attack you at the same time.
member.remarks: Having this option enabled keeps you in combat at all times, sometimes making it impossible to turn in quests.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AggroMonsters
member.summary: Determines whether all monsters in the room should be provoked. They will all attack you at the same time.
member.remarks: Having this option enabled could keep you in combat at all times, sometimes making it impossible to turn in quests.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AttackWithoutTarget
member.summary: Setting this to true will make it use the skills even without target. Use with caution.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AutoRelogin
member.summary: Enables the auto re-login feature. If enabled, when the player is logged out of the game, they will automatically be logged back in with the configured username and password, to the configured server.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.AutoReloginAny
member.summary: Re-logs into any server that wasn't the last one. This ensures the re-log is successful.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.RetryRelogin
member.summary: Whether it should try certain amount of times () to re-login.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.CustomGuild
member.summary: Sets a persistent, custom guild name (client side).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.CustomName
member.summary: Sets a persistent, custom player name (client side).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.DisableCollisions
member.summary: Disables all collisions in the game.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.DisableDeathAds
member.summary: Disables the death AD.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.DisableFX
member.summary: Disables all player combat animations (improves frame-rate).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.MaximumTries
member.summary: Maximum tries for Ensure (like ) methods.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.ActionDelay
member.summary: Delay in milliseconds that the bot will take to perform some actions.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.GuildColor
member.summary: Sets the color of your guild name with HEX (0xFFFFFF)

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.HidePlayers
member.summary: When enabled, all player avatars are hidden.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.HuntBuffer
member.summary: How many kills hunt should wait for before picking up drops.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.HuntDelay
member.summary: The minimum time between jumping between rooms when hunting for enemies (in milliseconds). The default is 1000ms.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.InfiniteRange
member.summary: Enabling this option allows you to attack targets from any range (without moving).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.LagKiller
member.summary: Disables drawing the world to (somewhat) reduce lag and CPU usage.
member.remarks: It is much more effective to minimize the game to reduce CPU usage than to enable this option. For the lowest CPU usage, try both.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.HeadlessMode
member.summary: Suspends DWM repaints and hides the game renderer completely to drastically drop CPU/GPU usage, without pausing ActionScript logic.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.LoadTimeout
member.summary: The time in ms that the game is allowed to load before logging the user out (triggering a re-login if enabled).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.ReloginServer
member.summary: The server to re-login to.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.Magnetise
member.summary: When enabled, this will cause all targeted monsters to teleport to you.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.NameColor
member.summary: Sets the color of your name with HEX (0xFFFFFF)

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.PrivateRooms
member.summary: Determines whether to join only private rooms or not.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.RestPackets
member.summary: A rest packet will be sent every second, causing the player to heal when not in combat.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.SafeRelogin
member.summary: When enabled, there will be a 1-minute 15-second delay before the player is re-logged in.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.SafeTimings
member.summary: When safe timings are enabled, the bot will wait for any action called to be completed with a timeout of (generally) 5 seconds (i.e. picking a drop) before continuing execution. It is strongly recommended that this is turned on.
member.remarks: This option does not ensure actions are carried out successfully, as it is quite possible that the 5-second timeout is reached before an action is completed.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.SetFPS
member.summary: Changes game maximum FPS (frames per second)

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.ShowFPS
member.summary: Whether to show the in-game FPS counter

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.SkipCutscenes
member.summary: Determines whether cutscenes should be skipped.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.WalkSpeed
member.summary: An option to constantly modify the player's walk speed (the ScriptManager's timer thread will update the in-game value).

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.PrivateNumber
member.summary: Delay in milliseconds that the bot will take to perform some actions.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.JoinMapTries
member.summary: How many tries the bot will make to join a map.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.QuestAcceptAndCompleteTries
member.summary: How many tries the bot will make when trying to accept/complete a quest.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.ReloginTries
member.summary: How many tries the bot will make when using

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.HuntPriority
member.summary: The priority mode for hunting.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.Reset
member.summary: Resets the options to the user defined defaults. If no user settings are found, uses the application default values.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.ResetToDefault
member.summary: Resets the options to the application default values.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptOption.Save
member.summary: Saves the current options to the user settings.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptQuest
member.summary: Defines the contract for managing, tracking, and automating quest-related operations for a player, including accepting, completing, registering, and querying quests.
member.remarks: The interface provides methods and properties to interact with quests in a session, such as loading quest data, checking quest status, and automating quest completion. It supports both individual and batch operations, and exposes collections for active, completed, and cached quests. Implementations should notify property changes via .

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend
member.summary: Defines methods for sending packets and chat messages to the server or client, including support for various message types and packet spamming operations.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide mechanisms to send both server-bound and client-simulated packets, as well as specialized chat messages with different formatting and colors. Some methods may have side effects or risks, such as triggering server moderation actions if misused. Asynchronous packet spamming methods allow repeated sending until cancellation is requested. Use caution when sending non-client-sided messages, as they may be tracked by the server.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.Packet(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends the specified packet to the server.
member.param: Packet to be sent.
Type of the packet being sent (String, Json).
member.remarks: Be careful when using this method. Incorrect use of this method may cause you to be kicked (or banned, although very unlikely).

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientPacket(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends the specified packet to the client (simulates a response as if the server sent the packet).
member.param: Packet to be sent.
Type of the packet (xml, json, str).

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientEvent(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with gray color surrounded by asterisks (*) (event messages).
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientGuild(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with green color (guild messages).
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientModerator(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with golden color (moderator messages).
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientServer(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with blue color (server messages).
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientWarning(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with red color (warning messages).
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientWhisper(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with purple color (whisper messages).
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSend.ClientZone(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sends a client side packet message with default chat color.
member.param: Message to be sent.
Name of who sent the message.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptServers
member.summary: Defines the contract for managing game server connections, authentication, and re-login operations within a scriptable environment.
member.remarks: This interface provides methods and properties for handling server lists, connection state, login credentials, and automated re-login scenarios. Implementations are expected to support property change notifications via . Thread safety and specific error handling behaviors depend on the concrete implementation.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptShop
member.summary: Defines the contract for interacting with in-game shops, including loading shops, retrieving shop information, and buying or selling items.
member.remarks: The interface provides methods and properties for managing shop sessions, accessing available items, and performing purchase or sale operations. Implementations should ensure that shop loading and item transactions are handled according to game rules and constraints. Some methods may trigger shop loading or bypass standard timing checks; refer to individual member documentation for details and usage considerations.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptShop.Name

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill
member.summary: Defines the contract for managing and executing skills using configurable providers, intervals, and usage modes. Supports loading, starting, and stopping skill execution, as well as customizing skill behavior for different classes and scenarios.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow for flexible skill automation by supporting both default and override providers, advanced skill loading from configuration, and runtime control over skill execution. Thread safety and provider management may vary by implementation. The interface is designed to be used in bot frameworks or automation systems that require dynamic skill usage strategies.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill.BaseProvider
member.summary: The default provider used if no override is set.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill.OverrideProvider
member.summary: This provider is always used over any set through SetProvider.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill.SkillTimeout
member.summary: The timeout in multiples of milliseconds before skipping the current unavailable skill when using .

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill.SkillInterval
member.summary: The interval, in milliseconds, at which to use skills.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill.TimerRunning
member.summary: Determines whether the skill thread is currently running.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptSkill.SkillUseMode
member.summary:
The way the bot will use skills:
If not set to skip, will wait for the skill to be available and then use it.
Whenever a skill is available it will use the skill, if not will skip it.

member.returns: if the skill was used, if the skill was not used.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptWait
member.summary: Defines an interface for managing and customizing wait operations in scripts, including configurable timeouts and polling intervals for various in-game actions and conditions.
member.remarks: The interface provides a standardized way to control how scripts wait for specific game events or state changes, such as item pickups, quest completions, or cooldowns. It allows fine-grained adjustment of polling intervals and timeout behavior for different categories of actions, supporting both synchronous and asynchronous waiting patterns. Implementations may use these settings to optimize script responsiveness and reliability.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptWait.WAIT_SLEEP
member.summary: The duration, in milliseconds, for which the thread will sleep before re-checking whether the awaited condition is met.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptWait.OverrideTimeout
member.summary: Whether to override all Wait timeouts with each of the defined ActionTimeouts. By default, they will not change the behaviour of the bot. Methods mentioned in each ActionTimeout are used when is enabled.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager
member.summary: Defines the contract for managing script execution, compilation, and configuration within an application.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide mechanisms to start, compile, and manage scripts, as well as to monitor script status and respond to property changes. This interface extends both for script state information and to support data binding scenarios.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.ScriptCts
member.summary: Gets the cancellation token source used to signal cancellation for the script operation.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.ScriptPaused
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the script is currently paused.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.ScriptPauseEvent
member.summary: Event used to pause and resume the script execution thread.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.StartScript
member.summary: Asynchronously starts the script and returns any exception that occurs during execution.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains an exception if the script fails; otherwise, null if the script completes successfully.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.Compile(System.String)
member.summary: Compiles the specified source code and returns the resulting object representation.
member.param: The source code to compile. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: An object representing the compiled form of the source code, or null if compilation fails.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.LoadScriptConfig(System.Object)
member.summary: Loads the specified script configuration into the current context.
member.param: An object representing the script configuration to load. Can be null to clear the current configuration.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptManager.SetLoadedScript(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the path of the currently loaded script.
member.param: The file system path to the script to be marked as loaded. Cannot be null or empty.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus
member.summary: Represents the status and control interface for a running script, providing properties to query script state and methods to manage script execution.
member.remarks: Use this interface to monitor the execution state of a script and to perform actions such as restarting or stopping the script. Implementations may provide additional behavior or state information relevant to script management.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.ShouldExit
member.summary: Whether the current script should terminate.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.ScriptRunning
member.summary: Whether the script is running.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.LoadedScript
member.summary: Path to the currently loaded script

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.CompiledScript
member.summary: The last script compiled.

member.name: P:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.Config
member.summary: Gets or sets the script configuration options for this instance.
member.remarks: Use this property to provide or retrieve custom script options that influence script execution or behavior. The value may be null if no configuration is specified.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.RestartScriptAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously restarts the currently running script.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous restart operation.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.StartScript
member.summary: Asynchronously starts a script.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous start operation.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.SetLoadedScript(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the path to for you to start a script with .

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IScriptStatus.StopScript(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Asynchronously stops the currently running script.
member.param: true to trigger the script stopping event before stopping the script; otherwise, false.
member.returns: A ValueTask that represents the asynchronous stop operation.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IClientFilesService
member.summary: Defines methods for creating required directories and files for client operations.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface are responsible for ensuring that the necessary directory structure and files exist before performing file-related operations. The specific directories and files created may vary depending on the implementation. Callers should consult the documentation of the implementing class for details about the created resources and any associated side effects.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IClientFilesService.CreateDirectories
member.summary: Creates all required directories for the current operation if they do not already exist.
member.remarks: This method ensures that any necessary directory structure is present before performing file operations. If the directories already exist, no action is taken.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IClientFilesService.CreateFiles
member.summary: Creates one or more files as defined by the implementing class.
member.remarks: The specific files created and their locations depend on the implementation. Callers should refer to the documentation of the implementing class for details about which files are created and any side effects.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IClipboardService
member.summary: Defines a service for interacting with the system clipboard, enabling applications to set and retrieve text and data in various formats.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide clipboard functionality that may be platform-specific. Methods support both standard text operations and custom data formats, allowing for flexible data exchange between applications.

member.remarks: If data for the specified format already exists, it will be overwritten. The format string is typically a predefined or custom format identifier recognized by the application or system.

The data object to associate with the specified format. Can be null to remove existing data for the format.

member.summary: Retrieves the data associated with the specified format.

member.returns: An object containing the data in the specified format, or null if the data is not available in that format.

member.name: M:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IClipboardService.GetText

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.IMapService
member.summary: Defines the contract for a map service that provides access to map-related information, cell and pad data, and navigation operations within a mapping context.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface are expected to support property change notifications via the interface. The interface exposes properties for retrieving map and cell information, as well as methods for performing navigation actions such as travel and jump. Consumers should refer to the specific implementation for details on supported operations and any additional constraints.

member.name: T:Skua.Core.Interfaces.ISkillProvider

member.remarks: Implementations of this interface are responsible for providing logic to select and trigger skills based on game state, as well as handling events such as target changes and player death. This interface is intended for use in systems where automated or programmatic skill usage is required.
